summary: Add the MVP Spec generation slice with a Codex-ready Markdown agent, persisted MvpSpec API, and copyable detail-page UI.
acceptance_criteria: An MVP Spec Agent exists at agents/mvp-spec-agent.ts and turns a persisted opportunity detail view model into deterministic, Codex-ready Markdown containing page structure, form fields, API routes, monetization entry points, risk notes, acceptance criteria, and kill criteria.

The agent supports injected LLM/safe-JSON dependencies or deterministic local generation without requiring secrets, provider setup, network access, package changes, or environment reads in this slice.

A POST /api/opportunities/[id]/mvp-spec route exists and loads one persisted Opportunity, generates Markdown, saves it to the existing MvpSpec table, and returns a safe success envelope with the persisted Markdown and timestamps.

The API safely handles invalid/missing opportunity ids, agent validation failures, and unexpected errors without leaking provider errors, stack traces, secrets, raw prompt payloads, or internal database details.

The /opportunities/[id] detail UI exposes a restrained Generate MVP Spec and Copy Markdown experience. It shows loading, error, empty, and already-generated states, and copy behavior works from the generated Markdown without adding unrelated status or scan behavior.
Reopening an opportunity detail can display an already-generated persisted MVP Spec when one exists.

Focused automated tests cover agent Markdown content, persistence/upsert behavior, API validation and sanitized errors, persisted spec serialization, copy panel rendering where practical, and non-goal boundaries.
README documents the MVP Spec slice, local data dependency, validation commands, local single-admin assumption, and explicit non-goals.

No Prisma schema migration, package dependency, real provider setup, env secret loading, scan orchestration, scoring formula change, auth/session system, deployment, GitHub Actions, status workflow expansion, or public release behavior is implemented.
notes: Derived from docs/09_codex_implementation_tasks.md Task 14, docs/11_development_plan.md M6, docs/05_ui_ux_plan.md Copy MVP Spec, and docs/10_acceptance_criteria_and_test_plan.md Flow 5.
The Prisma MvpSpec model already exists; this task must use it without schema or migration changes.
systems_architect is required because this adds an agent/API/persistence/UI boundary.
domain_designer is required because generated Markdown must be useful for Codex while preserving risk and monetization caveats.
security_reviewer is required because this route persists generated text and may later be connected to real model providers.
non_goals: No Prisma schema changes, migrations, seed changes, package changes, real provider setup, LLM credential setup, env secret loading, auth/session, deployment, or GitHub Actions.

No scan orchestration changes, SERP provider changes, scoring formula changes, Radar Task CRUD changes, status workflow expansion, discard reason workflow, built-performance tracking, or recommendation-learning loop.
No public sharing, billing, telemetry, multi-user ownership, irreversible deletes, external file export, or deployment policy.
No broad rewrite of Opportunity Detail, Dashboard, Opportunity List, existing status API, or existing scan APIs beyond the minimal hooks needed to display/generate/copy MVP Spec.
